rawText:
The peer review system — the process by which scientific manuscripts are evaluated by independent experts before publication — is often described as the cornerstone of scientific quality control, the mechanism that separates rigorous research from speculation and ensures that the knowledge base upon which society depends is sound, reliable, and trustworthy. This description is not wrong, exactly, but it is dangerously incomplete, because it implies a level of reliability and consistency that the system has never, in practice, been able to deliver. The reality, as a growing body of evidence makes clear, is that peer review is a deeply imperfect process — one that catches many errors but misses many others, that is vulnerable to bias, manipulation, and outright fraud, and that is currently straining under pressures so severe that some observers have begun to wonder whether it can survive in anything resembling its present form.

The basic mechanics of peer review are straightforward, at least in principle. A researcher submits a manuscript to a journal. The journal's editor conducts an initial screening and, if the manuscript passes this threshold, sends it to two or three independent experts — the "peers" — who evaluate the work's methodology, analysis, conclusions, and significance. The reviewers provide written feedback, typically recommending acceptance, revision, or rejection, and the editor makes a final decision based on their recommendations. The entire process is, in most cases, anonymous: the reviewers do not know the authors' identities (in double-blind review) or, more commonly, the authors do not know the reviewers' identities (in single-blind review). The process is unpaid; reviewers donate their time and expertise as a form of professional service, contributing to the collective enterprise of science.

In practice, however, every step of this process is fraught with difficulties that the textbook description obscures. The first and most fundamental problem is the recruitment of reviewers. As the volume of scientific publication has exploded — the ten largest publishers alone produced approximately 2.82 million journal articles in 2022, and the number has only grown since — the demand for peer reviewers has far outstripped the supply. A 2025 article in Nature, titled "The peer-review crisis: how to fix an overloaded system," documented that journal editors are now routinely sending up to thirty-five invitations just to secure two reviewers for a single manuscript, a ratio that reflects not only the sheer volume of submissions but also the growing reluctance of scientists to take on unpaid review work when their own careers are judged almost entirely on their publication records rather than on their service to the scientific community.

The quality of reviews, even when reviewers can be found, is enormously variable. Some reviews are meticulous, constructive, and genuinely improve the manuscript; others are perfunctory, dismissive, or uninformed. The variability is partly a consequence of the voluntary, unpaid nature of the work — reviewers who are rushed, overcommitted, or simply uninterested in the manuscript they have been assigned are unlikely to produce careful, thoughtful evaluations — and partly a consequence of the lack of training or standards for the review process itself. A 2025 analysis published in PMC noted that reviewer fatigue not only compromises the quality of feedback but can lead to the failure to identify critical flaws in a paper, allowing methodologically unsound or even fraudulent research to pass through the system and into the published literature.

The problem of bias in peer review has been documented extensively and takes multiple forms. There is confirmation bias, in which reviewers are more likely to approve manuscripts that support their own theoretical positions and more likely to reject those that challenge them. There is prestige bias, in which manuscripts from well-known researchers at elite institutions receive more favorable reviews than comparable manuscripts from less prominent authors. There is gender bias, in which female researchers receive harsher reviews and longer time-to-decision than their male counterparts. And there is geographic bias, in which researchers from low- and middle-income countries face higher rejection rates, even after controlling for the quality of their work. These biases are not the result of deliberate malice — they are, for the most part, unconscious and systemic — but their cumulative effect is to distort the scientific record in ways that privilege certain voices, methodologies, and conclusions over others.

The crisis of fraud and manipulation in peer review has reached a scale that would have been unimaginable a decade ago. The entities that enable scientific fraud at scale — organizations known as "paper mills," which produce fabricated manuscripts on demand and sell authorship positions to researchers under pressure to publish — have grown into a sophisticated industry. A 2025 study published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS) found that fraudulent publications are doubling every 1.5 years, a rate ten times faster than the growth of legitimate scientific literature. These paper mills have learned to game the peer review system by infiltrating journals' editorial boards, compromising the identities of legitimate reviewers, and submitting fake reviews that recommend acceptance of fraudulent manuscripts. The scale of the problem is staggering: more than ten thousand articles were retracted by Hindawi, a Wiley-owned publisher, in 2023 alone, following the discovery of industrialized peer-review manipulation.

The retraction crisis, as it has come to be known, is both a symptom and a consequence of peer review's failures. When a published paper is found to contain fabricated data, plagiarized text, or other serious integrity violations, the standard remedy is retraction — the formal withdrawal of the paper from the scientific record. But retractions are blunt instruments, applied after the damage has been done, and the process is slow, inconsistent, and often resisted by the authors and institutions involved. An analysis by Retraction Watch and the research integrity community revealed that forty-five editors implicated in a single fraudulent network edited only 1.3 percent of all articles in the affected journals but were responsible for 30.2 percent of all retracted articles — a stunning concentration of malfeasance that illustrates how a small number of compromised individuals can inflict disproportionate damage on the integrity of the scientific literature.

The emergence of artificial intelligence as both a threat to and a potential savior of peer review is one of the most consequential developments in scientific publishing. On the threat side, generative AI tools have made it easier than ever to produce plausible-looking but entirely fabricated manuscripts, complete with realistic data, coherent methodology sections, and properly formatted references. An AI tool that scans manuscript titles and abstracts has flagged more than two hundred and fifty thousand cancer studies alone that bear textual similarities to articles known to have been produced by paper mills — a number that suggests the true scale of the problem is far larger than the retraction statistics indicate. On the potential-savior side, major publishers including Springer Nature, Elsevier, and Wiley are now piloting AI systems for manuscript screening, plagiarism detection, and statistical verification, tools that could dramatically improve the efficiency and consistency of the review process if they prove reliable.

The question of whether peer reviewers should be paid is one of the most contentious issues in the current reform debate. The traditional model, in which review is an unpaid service, was tenable in an era when the volume of submissions was manageable and the professional incentives were aligned — when reviewing was seen as a privilege rather than a burden, and when the community of researchers in any given field was small enough that reciprocity was the natural norm. That era has passed. The sheer volume of submissions, the pressure to publish, and the absence of any career reward for reviewing have created a situation in which the system depends on goodwill that is rapidly depleting. A 2025 article in PMC argued forcefully for the professionalization of peer review, proposing that reviewers be compensated as "professional referees" with formal training, certification, and remuneration — a model that would increase the cost of publishing but might also increase the quality and reliability of the review process.

The concept of "open" or "transparent" peer review has gained considerable traction as an alternative to the traditional anonymous model. In transparent peer review, the reviewers' identities are disclosed to the authors (and sometimes to the public), and the review reports are published alongside the final article, allowing readers to see not just the finished product but the evaluative process that shaped it. Proponents argue that transparency reduces bias, increases accountability, and produces more constructive reviews, because reviewers who know their comments will be public are less likely to be dismissive or reckless. Critics counter that transparency may discourage candid criticism, particularly from junior researchers who fear retaliation from senior colleagues, and that the public scrutiny of reviews could make the already unattractive task of reviewing even less appealing.

The reproducibility crisis, which has been particularly acute in psychology, biomedicine, and the social sciences, is intimately connected to the limitations of peer review. If the purpose of peer review is to ensure that published research is sound, then the finding that a significant proportion of published results cannot be replicated — estimates range from thirty to seventy percent, depending on the field — represents a devastating indictment of the system's effectiveness. Peer reviewers are not, of course, expected to replicate the experiments they evaluate; they are expected to assess the methodology, the statistical analysis, and the logic of the conclusions. But the reproducibility crisis suggests that these assessments are often insufficient, that reviewers routinely approve papers with underpowered studies, inappropriate statistical methods, or selective reporting of results — flaws that are sometimes difficult to detect from a manuscript alone but that become apparent when the research is subjected to independent replication.

The geographic dimension of peer review's challenges deserves attention, because the system's failures are not distributed equally around the world. China's dominance in AI-related retractions — with five hundred and fifty-one publications retracted, far exceeding any other country — has drawn particular scrutiny and has raised questions about the institutional pressures that drive publication fraud. In China, as in several other countries, academic promotion, funding, and even housing subsidies are tied directly to publication metrics, creating incentives for fraud that overwhelm the ethical norms that peer review depends on. The problem is not unique to China — publication pressure exists everywhere — but the scale and visibility of the Chinese retraction numbers have focused international attention on the systemic conditions that produce fraud, and on the inadequacy of peer review as a defense against it.

The future of peer review will almost certainly involve a hybrid model that combines human judgment with AI-assisted screening, transparent reporting with some degree of reviewer anonymity, and professional remuneration with continued reliance on voluntary community service. The American Psychological Association's 2025 initiative to strengthen and expand access to scholarly publishing reflects this hybrid vision, emphasizing that the goal is not to replace peer review but to adapt it to the realities of twenty-first-century scientific publishing — a landscape characterized by exponential growth in submissions, increasing sophistication of fraud, declining reviewer availability, and intensifying public demand for scientific reliability.

What the current crisis reveals, more than anything, is that peer review was never designed to do what we have asked it to do. It was designed as a collegial mechanism for improving manuscripts within small, trust-based communities of specialists. It was not designed to serve as the primary defense against industrial-scale fraud, the sole guarantor of scientific reliability, or the universal standard of quality across every discipline, language, and culture on earth. The demands we have placed on the system have exceeded its capacity, and the result is a growing gap between the ideal of peer review — the careful, expert, disinterested evaluation of scientific work — and the reality, which is often rushed, inconsistent, and vulnerable to the very problems it is supposed to prevent.

Reforming peer review will require not just new tools and new incentives but a fundamental reconceptualization of its role in the scientific enterprise. Peer review is one component of a much larger system of quality control — a system that also includes replication, post-publication review, statistical auditing, and the collective scrutiny of the scientific community over time — and expecting it to bear the entire weight of scientific integrity is neither fair nor realistic. The path forward lies not in defending peer review as it is or in abolishing it in frustration, but in strengthening it where it works, supplementing it where it does not, and being honest about its limitations — the same kind of honesty that science, at its best, demands of everything else.

overview.title: How Peer Review Works — And When It Doesn't

sentences.text: Before a science paper is published, other scientists read it to check if it is good — this is called peer review.
sentences.translation: 과학 논문이 출판되기 전에, 다른 과학자들이 읽고 좋은지 확인해요 — 이것을 동료 심사라고 해요.

sentences.text: But there are so many papers now that it is hard to find enough people to check them all.
sentences.translation: 하지만 지금은 논문이 너무 많아서 다 확인할 사람을 찾기 어려워요.

sentences.text: Some people cheat by making fake papers and tricking the system into publishing them.
sentences.translation: 어떤 사람들은 가짜 논문을 만들고 시스템을 속여서 출판하는 부정행위를 해요.

sentences.text: Scientists also have biases, so they might like papers that agree with them more than papers that disagree.
sentences.translation: 과학자들도 편견이 있어서, 자기와 동의하는 논문을 동의하지 않는 논문보다 더 좋아할 수 있어요.

sentences.text: Computers and AI might help catch mistakes and fake papers in the future.
sentences.translation: 미래에는 컴퓨터와 AI가 실수와 가짜 논문을 잡는 데 도움이 될 수 있어요.

sentences.text: Peer review is not perfect, but people are working to make it better.
sentences.translation: 동료 심사는 완벽하지 않지만, 사람들이 더 좋게 만들려고 노력하고 있어요.

sentences.text: The article explains that peer review — the system where experts evaluate scientific papers before publication — is widely trusted but deeply flawed.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 동료 심사 — 전문가들이 출판 전에 과학 논문을 평가하는 제도 — 가 널리 신뢰받지만 심각한 결함이 있다고 설명해요.

sentences.text: The explosion of scientific publications has made it extremely difficult to recruit enough qualified reviewers, with editors sending dozens of invitations to secure even two.
sentences.translation: 과학 출판물의 폭발적 증가로 충분한 자격을 갖춘 심사자를 모집하기가 극히 어려워졌고, 편집자들은 두 명을 확보하기 위해 수십 건의 초대를 보내야 해요.

sentences.text: "Paper mills" — factories that produce fake scientific papers — have become a sophisticated industry that games the peer review system through infiltration and fake reviews.
sentences.translation: 가짜 과학 논문을 생산하는 공장인 "논문 공장"이 침투와 가짜 심사를 통해 동료 심사 제도를 악용하는 정교한 산업이 되었어요.

sentences.text: Multiple forms of bias — confirmation, prestige, gender, and geographic — systematically distort which research gets published and which does not.
sentences.translation: 확증 편향, 명성 편향, 성별 편향, 지리적 편향 등 다양한 형태의 편향이 어떤 연구가 출판되고 어떤 연구가 출판되지 않는지를 체계적으로 왜곡해요.

sentences.text: Proposed reforms include paying reviewers, making the review process transparent, and using AI for manuscript screening and fraud detection.
sentences.translation: 제안된 개혁에는 심사자에게 보수를 지급하고, 심사 과정을 투명하게 하고, 원고 선별과 부정행위 탐지에 AI를 활용하는 것이 포함돼요.

sentences.text: The article concludes that peer review should be strengthened and supplemented rather than defended as-is or abolished entirely.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 동료 심사를 현 상태로 옹호하거나 완전히 폐지하기보다 강화하고 보완해야 한다고 결론지어요.

sentences.text: This investigative essay systematically deconstructs the peer review system by cataloguing its structural vulnerabilities — reviewer scarcity, quality variance, systemic bias, and industrial-scale fraud — while resisting both uncritical defense and nihilistic dismissal.
sentences.translation: 이 탐사 에세이는 심사자 부족, 품질 편차, 체계적 편향, 산업 규모 부정행위 등 구조적 취약성을 목록화하여 동료 심사 제도를 체계적으로 해체하면서, 무비판적 옹호와 허무주의적 폄하 모두를 거부해요.

sentences.text: The paper mill phenomenon is presented as a market failure: publication-contingent career incentives create demand for fraudulent output that overwhelms the volunteer-based quality control infrastructure.
sentences.translation: 논문 공장 현상은 시장 실패로 제시돼요: 출판 의존적 경력 인센티브가 자원봉사 기반 품질 관리 인프라를 압도하는 부정 산출물에 대한 수요를 만들어요.

sentences.text: The reproducibility crisis is framed as peer review's ultimate stress test — if thirty to seventy percent of published results fail replication, the system's core epistemic function is compromised.
sentences.translation: 재현성 위기는 동료 심사의 궁극적 스트레스 테스트로 틀 지어져요 — 출판된 결과의 30~70%가 재현에 실패한다면, 제도의 핵심적 인식론적 기능이 손상된 것이에요.

sentences.text: AI is positioned dialectically — simultaneously the greatest emerging threat (generative fabrication) and the most promising countermeasure (automated screening and statistical auditing).
sentences.translation: AI는 변증법적으로 위치해요 — 동시에 가장 큰 신흥 위협(생성적 조작)이자 가장 유망한 대응책(자동 선별 및 통계 감사)이에요.

sentences.text: The essay's central rhetorical move is reframing: peer review fails not because the system is broken but because we have asked it to perform functions — fraud detection, universal quality certification — it was never architected to serve.
sentences.translation: 에세이의 핵심 수사적 전략은 재구성이에요: 동료 심사가 실패하는 것은 제도가 망가져서가 아니라, 부정행위 탐지, 보편적 품질 인증 등 원래 수행하도록 설계되지 않은 기능을 요구했기 때문이에요.

sentences.text: The conclusion invokes science's own epistemic standard — honesty about limitations — as the principle that should govern reform, creating a self-referential ethical closure.
sentences.translation: 결론은 한계에 대한 정직성이라는 과학 자체의 인식론적 기준을 개혁을 이끌어야 할 원칙으로 환기하여, 자기참조적 윤리적 마무리를 만들어요.

sentences.original: The peer review system — the process by which scientific manuscripts are evaluated by independent experts before publication — is often described as the cornerstone of scientific quality control, the mechanism that separates rigorous research from speculation and ensures that the knowledge base upon which society depends is sound, reliable, and trustworthy. This description is not wrong, exactly, but it is dangerously incomplete, because it implies a level of reliability and consistency that the system has never, in practice, been able to deliver. The reality, as a growing body of evidence makes clear, is that peer review is a deeply imperfect process — one that catches many errors but misses many others, that is vulnerable to bias, manipulation, and outright fraud, and that is currently straining under pressures so severe that some observers have begun to wonder whether it can survive in anything resembling its present form.
sentences.translation: 동료 심사 제도 — 과학 원고가 출판 전에 독립적 전문가들에 의해 평가되는 과정 — 는 흔히 과학적 품질 관리의 초석, 즉 엄밀한 연구를 추측으로부터 분리하고 사회가 의존하는 지식 기반이 건전하고 신뢰할 수 있으며 믿을 만하다는 것을 보장하는 메커니즘으로 묘사된다. 이 묘사가 정확히 틀린 것은 아니지만, 위험할 정도로 불완전하다. 왜냐하면 이 제도가 실제로는 결코 제공할 수 없었던 수준의 신뢰성과 일관성을 암시하기 때문이다. 현실은, 점점 늘어나는 증거가 분명히 보여주듯, 동료 심사가 깊이 불완전한 과정이라는 것이다 — 많은 오류를 잡지만 다른 많은 오류를 놓치고, 편견과 조작과 명백한 사기에 취약하며, 현재 일부 관찰자들이 현재 형태와 유사한 어떤 것이든 살아남을 수 있을지 의문을 품기 시작할 정도로 심각한 압력 속에서 허덕이고 있다.

patterns.explanation: 먼저 통상적 묘사를 인정한 뒤 양보적 전환(not wrong but incomplete)으로 핵심 비판을 도입하는 학술적 도입 패턴. 권위 있는 비평 글에서 자주 사용된다.
examples: The free market is often described as the engine of innovation. This description is not wrong, exactly, but it is dangerously incomplete, because it ignores the role of public investment in foundational research.

sentences.notes: 글 전체의 핵심 논지를 도입하는 서론부. 세 문장으로 구성되며 "not wrong but incomplete"라는 양보적 비판 구조가 이후 전개의 기조를 설정한다. 마지막 문장에서 세 개의 "that" 관계절이 병렬되어 동료 심사의 세 가지 결함(오류 누락, 편견 취약성, 체제적 압력)을 체계적으로 나열한다.

The entire process is, in most cases, anonymous: the reviewers do not know the authors' identities (in double-blind review) or, more commonly, the authors do not know the reviewers' identities (in single-blind review). The process is unpaid; reviewers donate their time and expertise as a form of professional service, contributing to the collective enterprise of science.
sentences.translation: 동료 심사의 기본 작동 방식은 원칙적으로는 간단명료하다. 연구자가 학술지에 원고를 제출한다. 학술지 편집자가 초기 심사를 실시하고, 원고가 이 기준을 통과하면 두세 명의 독립적 전문가 — "동료" — 에게 보내어 연구의 방법론, 분석, 결론, 중요성을 평가하게 한다. 심사자들은 서면 피드백을 제공하며, 보통 수락, 수정, 또는 거부를 권고하고, 편집자가 그들의 권고에 기반하여 최종 결정을 내린다. 전체 과정은 대부분의 경우 익명이다: 심사자가 저자의 신원을 모르거나(이중 맹검 심사) 더 흔하게는 저자가 심사자의 신원을 모른다(단일 맹검 심사). 이 과정은 무급이다; 심사자들은 전문적 봉사의 형태로 시간과 전문 지식을 기부하여 과학이라는 집단적 사업에 기여한다.

grammarElements.text: The basic mechanics of peer review
grammarElements.label: 주어 (명사구)

grammarElements.text: at least in principle
grammarElements.label: 삽입 부사구 (제한적 양보)

grammarElements.text: if the manuscript passes this threshold
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (if + 현재형)

grammarElements.text: the "peers" — who evaluate the work's methodology, analysis, conclusions, and significance
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 비제한적 관계절 + 병렬 목적어

vocabulary.example: Understanding the mechanics of how a bill becomes a law is essential for civic education.
The manuscript failed to meet the threshold for publication.
She spent three months revising the manuscript before resubmission.

expressions.usage: 이론과 현실의 차이를 암시하며, 뒤에 실제 어려움이 따라올 것을 예고하는 표현
과학 연구가 공동체적 협력임을 강조하는 격식적 표현

patterns.pattern: The basic mechanics of [X] are straightforward, at least in principle. [Step-by-step description]. The process is [characteristic]; [elaboration].
patterns.explanation: "원칙적으로 간단하다"는 양보로 시작한 뒤 단계별 설명을 나열하고, 세미콜론으로 보충 설명을 덧붙이는 설명적 패턴. 이후 "실제로는 다르다"라는 전환이 암시된다.
examples: The basic mechanics of voting are straightforward, at least in principle. A citizen registers, receives a ballot, and marks their preferences. The process is designed to be simple; every eligible adult should be able to participate without special training.

sentences.notes: 동료 심사의 기본 절차를 단계별로 설명하는 해설 문단. "at least in principle"이라는 유보적 표현이 다음 문단에서 현실의 어려움을 대비시킬 복선 역할을 한다. 짧은 문장들이 연속되어 절차의 명확성을 형식적으로도 반영한다. 이중/단일 맹검(double-blind/single-blind)이라는 전문 용어가 괄호로 소개된다.

koreanAlignment.en: The basic mechanics of peer review are straightforward, at least in principle
koreanAlignment.ko: 동료 심사의 기본 작동 방식은 원칙적으로는 간단명료하다

koreanAlignment.en: sends it to two or three independent experts — the "peers"
koreanAlignment.ko: 두세 명의 독립적 전문가 — "동료" — 에게 보낸다

koreanAlignment.en: typically recommending acceptance, revision, or rejection
koreanAlignment.ko: 보통 수락, 수정, 또는 거부를 권고하며

koreanAlignment.en: The process is unpaid; reviewers donate their time and expertise
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 과정은 무급이다; 심사자들은 시간과 전문 지식을 기부한다

pronunciationNotes: manuscript: /ˈmænjəskrɪpt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "매뉴스크립트"에 가깝다.
anonymous: /əˈnɒnɪməs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어나니머스"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: In practice, however, every step of this process is fraught with difficulties that the textbook description obscures. The first and most fundamental problem is the recruitment of reviewers. As the volume of scientific publication has exploded — the ten largest publishers alone produced approximately 2.82 million journal articles in 2022, and the number has only grown since — the demand for peer reviewers has far outstripped the supply. A 2025 article in Nature, titled "The peer-review crisis: how to fix an overloaded system," documented that journal editors are now routinely sending up to thirty-five invitations just to secure two reviewers for a single manuscript, a ratio that reflects not only the sheer volume of submissions but also the growing reluctance of scientists to take on unpaid review work when their own careers are judged almost entirely on their publication records rather than on their service to the scientific community.
sentences.translation: 그러나 실제로는 이 과정의 모든 단계가 교과서적 묘사가 감추는 어려움으로 가득 차 있다. 첫 번째이자 가장 근본적인 문제는 심사자 모집이다. 과학 출판의 양이 폭발적으로 증가하면서 — 10대 대형 출판사만 해도 2022년에 약 282만 건의 학술지 논문을 생산했으며, 그 수는 그 이후로 계속 증가해 왔다 — 동료 심사자에 대한 수요가 공급을 훨씬 능가했다. 2025년 Nature에 실린 "동료 심사 위기: 과부하 시스템을 어떻게 고칠 것인가"라는 제목의 논문은, 학술지 편집자들이 이제 단일 원고에 대해 두 명의 심사자를 확보하기 위해 일상적으로 최대 35건의 초대를 보내고 있다고 기록했는데, 이 비율은 제출 건수의 엄청난 규모뿐만 아니라 자신의 경력이 과학 공동체에 대한 봉사가 아니라 거의 전적으로 출판 실적으로 평가되는 상황에서 무보수 심사 업무를 맡으려는 과학자들의 증가하는 꺼림을 반영한다.

grammarElements.text: In practice, however
grammarElements.label: 부사구 + 전환 부사 (대조적 전환)

grammarElements.text: is fraught with difficulties
grammarElements.label: 동사구 (be fraught with = ~으로 가득 찬)

grammarElements.text: that the textbook description obscures
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (목적격)

grammarElements.text: not only ... but also
grammarElements.label: 상관 접속사 (병렬 강조)

vocabulary.example: The negotiations were fraught with tension from the very beginning.
Demand for the vaccine has far outstripped the available supply.
There is a growing reluctance among young doctors to work in rural areas.

expressions.usage: 상황이나 과정이 다양한 문제로 복잡하게 얽혀 있음을 나타내는 격식적 표현
수요-공급 불균형을 강조하는 경제/사회 분석 표현

patterns.pattern: In practice, however, [ideal process] is fraught with [problems] that [the standard account] obscures.
patterns.explanation: "실제로는 다르다"는 전환으로 앞서 제시된 이상적 묘사를 뒤집는 비판적 전환 패턴. "however"와 "fraught with"가 핵심 구조이다.
examples: In practice, however, every stage of the hiring process is fraught with biases that the official guidelines obscure.

sentences.notes: "In practice, however"로 시작하는 핵심 전환 문단. 앞 문단의 이상적 절차 설명을 "fraught with difficulties"로 뒤집으며, 구체적 통계(282만 건, 35건의 초대)가 문제의 규모를 입증한다. "not only ... but also" 구문이 양적 문제(제출량)와 질적 문제(과학자들의 꺼림)를 병렬한다.

koreanAlignment.en: In practice, however, every step of this process is fraught with difficulties
koreanAlignment.ko: 그러나 실제로는 이 과정의 모든 단계가 어려움으로 가득 차 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the demand for peer reviewers has far outstripped the supply
koreanAlignment.ko: 동료 심사자에 대한 수요가 공급을 훨씬 능가했다

koreanAlignment.en: sending up to thirty-five invitations just to secure two reviewers
koreanAlignment.ko: 두 명의 심사자를 확보하기 위해 최대 35건의 초대를 보내고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the growing reluctance of scientists to take on unpaid review work
koreanAlignment.ko: 무보수 심사 업무를 맡으려는 과학자들의 증가하는 꺼림

pronunciationNotes: fraught: /frɔːt/ — "프롯"에 가깝다. aught가 /ɔːt/로 발음.
outstripped: /aʊtˈstrɪpt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "아웃스트립트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The quality of reviews, even when reviewers can be found, is enormously variable. Some reviews are meticulous, constructive, and genuinely improve the manuscript; others are perfunctory, dismissive, or uninformed. The variability is partly a consequence of the voluntary, unpaid nature of the work — reviewers who are rushed, overcommitted, or simply uninterested in the manuscript they have been assigned are unlikely to produce careful, thoughtful evaluations — and partly a consequence of the lack of training or standards for the review process itself. A 2025 analysis published in PMC noted that reviewer fatigue not only compromises the quality of feedback but can lead to the failure to identify critical flaws in a paper, allowing methodologically unsound or even fraudulent research to pass through the system and into the published literature.
sentences.translation: 심사자를 찾을 수 있더라도, 심사의 질은 엄청나게 가변적이다. 일부 심사는 꼼꼼하고 건설적이며 원고를 진정으로 향상시키지만, 다른 심사는 형식적이거나 무시적이거나 정보가 부족하다. 이 가변성은 부분적으로는 업무의 자발적·무급 성격의 결과 — 바쁘거나, 과잉 업무에 시달리거나, 배정된 원고에 단순히 관심이 없는 심사자는 신중하고 사려 깊은 평가를 내놓기 어렵다 — 이며, 부분적으로는 심사 과정 자체에 대한 훈련이나 기준의 부재의 결과이다. 2025년 PMC에 게재된 분석은 심사자 피로가 피드백의 질을 저하시킬 뿐만 아니라 논문의 중대한 결함을 식별하지 못하는 실패로 이어질 수 있으며, 방법론적으로 부실하거나 심지어 부정한 연구가 시스템을 통과하여 출판된 문헌에 들어가도록 허용한다고 지적했다.

grammarElements.text: even when reviewers can be found
grammarElements.label: 양보 부사절 (even when + 수동태)

grammarElements.text: Some reviews are ... ; others are ...
grammarElements.label: 대조 병렬 (some/others + 세미콜론)

grammarElements.text: partly a consequence of ... — ... — and partly a consequence of ...
grammarElements.label: "partly ... and partly" 이중 원인 구조 + 대시 삽입

grammarElements.text: allowing methodologically unsound or even fraudulent research to pass through
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문 (allowing + 목적어 + to 부정사)

vocabulary.example: A meticulous review can save a paper from fundamental errors.
The review was so perfunctory that it missed obvious errors in the data.
Decision fatigue can reduce the quality of judgments made late in the day.

expressions.usage: 편차가 매우 크다는 것을 강조하는 학술적 표현
관리 체계를 거치는 과정을 비유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: Some [X] are [positive qualities]; others are [negative qualities]. The variability is partly a consequence of [cause A] and partly a consequence of [cause B].
patterns.explanation: "some/others" 대조와 "partly ... and partly" 이중 원인 분석을 결합하여 현상의 편차와 그 복합적 원인을 설명하는 분석 패턴.
examples: Some feedback is thoughtful and actionable; others are vague and unhelpful. The variability is partly a consequence of training differences and partly a consequence of time constraints.

sentences.notes: 심사 품질의 편차를 분석하는 문단. "some/others" 대조가 구체적 형용사 쌍(meticulous vs. perfunctory)으로 뒷받침되고, "partly ... and partly" 구문이 두 가지 원인(무급 노동, 훈련 부재)을 체계적으로 제시한다. "allowing ... to pass through"라는 결과 분사구문이 실질적 위험을 명시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The quality of reviews, even when reviewers can be found, is enormously variable
koreanAlignment.ko: 심사자를 찾을 수 있더라도, 심사의 질은 엄청나게 가변적이다

koreanAlignment.en: Some reviews are meticulous, constructive; others are perfunctory, dismissive
koreanAlignment.ko: 일부 심사는 꼼꼼하고 건설적이지만, 다른 심사는 형식적이거나 무시적이다

koreanAlignment.en: reviewer fatigue not only compromises the quality of feedback
koreanAlignment.ko: 심사자 피로가 피드백의 질을 저하시킬 뿐만 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: allowing methodologically unsound or even fraudulent research to pass through the system
koreanAlignment.ko: 방법론적으로 부실하거나 부정한 연구가 시스템을 통과하도록 허용한다

pronunciationNotes: meticulous: /mɪˈtɪkjələs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "미티큘러스"에 가깝다.
perfunctory: /pərˈfʌŋktəri/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "퍼펑토리"에 가깝다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조적 병렬과 이중 원인 분석 (contrastive parallelism with dual causation)

sentences.original: The problem of bias in peer review has been documented extensively and takes multiple forms. There is confirmation bias, in which reviewers are more likely to approve manuscripts that support their own theoretical positions and more likely to reject those that challenge them. There is prestige bias, in which manuscripts from well-known researchers at elite institutions receive more favorable reviews than comparable manuscripts from less prominent authors. There is gender bias, in which female researchers receive harsher reviews and longer time-to-decision than their male counterparts. And there is geographic bias, in which researchers from low- and middle-income countries face higher rejection rates, even after controlling for the quality of their work. These biases are not the result of deliberate malice — they are, for the most part, unconscious and systemic — but their cumulative effect is to distort the scientific record in ways that privilege certain voices, methodologies, and conclusions over others.
sentences.translation: 동료 심사에서의 편향 문제는 광범위하게 기록되어 왔으며 다양한 형태를 띤다. 확증 편향이 있는데, 이는 심사자가 자신의 이론적 입장을 뒷받침하는 원고를 승인할 가능성이 더 높고 그에 도전하는 원고를 거부할 가능성이 더 높은 것이다. 명성 편향이 있는데, 이는 엘리트 기관의 유명 연구자의 원고가 덜 저명한 저자의 비슷한 원고보다 더 호의적인 심사를 받는 것이다. 성별 편향이 있는데, 이는 여성 연구자가 남성 동료보다 더 가혹한 심사와 더 긴 결정 소요 시간을 받는 것이다. 그리고 지리적 편향이 있는데, 이는 저·중소득 국가의 연구자가 연구의 질을 통제한 후에도 더 높은 거부율에 직면하는 것이다. 이러한 편향들은 고의적 악의의 결과가 아니라 대부분 무의식적이고 구조적이지만, 그 누적 효과는 특정 목소리, 방법론, 결론을 다른 것들보다 우대하는 방식으로 과학적 기록을 왜곡하는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: has been documented extensively
grammarElements.label: 동사구 (현재완료 수동태 + 부사)

grammarElements.text: There is confirmation bias, in which ...
grammarElements.label: "There is" 존재구문 + 비제한적 관계절 (in which)

grammarElements.text: There is ... There is ... There is ... And there is ...
grammarElements.label: 열거적 병렬 (anaphora)

grammarElements.text: even after controlling for the quality of their work
grammarElements.label: 양보 부사절 (even after + 동명사)

vocabulary.example: Confirmation bias leads people to seek information that supports their existing beliefs.
The errors were attributed to negligence rather than deliberate malice.
The cumulative effect of small biases can produce significant inequality.

expressions.usage: 통계 연구에서 특정 변수의 영향을 제거하고 분석했음을 나타내는 학술적 표현
구조적 불평등이 특정 집단에 유리하게 작용함을 비판할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: There is [type A], in which [explanation]. There is [type B], in which [explanation]. And there is [type C], in which [explanation].
patterns.explanation: "There is"의 반복(수사적 반복법, anaphora)으로 같은 범주의 여러 유형을 열거하고, 각각 "in which" 관계절로 설명을 덧붙이는 분류 패턴.
examples: There is structural unemployment, in which entire industries decline. There is frictional unemployment, in which workers are temporarily between jobs. And there is cyclical unemployment, in which economic downturns reduce overall demand.

sentences.notes: 네 가지 편향(확증, 명성, 성별, 지리적)을 열거하는 분류 문단. "There is" 반복법(anaphora)이 각 편향 유형을 동등한 비중으로 제시하고, "in which"로 시작하는 관계절이 각각의 작동 방식을 설명한다. 마지막 문장의 "not the result of malice ... but"이 구조적 문제임을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The problem of bias in peer review has been documented extensively
koreanAlignment.ko: 동료 심사에서의 편향 문제는 광범위하게 기록되어 왔다

koreanAlignment.en: There is confirmation bias, in which reviewers are more likely to approve manuscripts that support their own theoretical positions
koreanAlignment.ko: 확증 편향이 있는데, 심사자가 자신의 이론적 입장을 뒷받침하는 원고를 승인할 가능성이 더 높다

koreanAlignment.en: These biases are not the result of deliberate malice
koreanAlignment.ko: 이러한 편향들은 고의적 악의의 결과가 아니다

koreanAlignment.en: their cumulative effect is to distort the scientific record
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 누적 효과는 과학적 기록을 왜곡하는 것이다

pronunciationNotes: bias: /ˈbaɪəs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "바이어스"에 가깝다. 복수형 biases는 /ˈbaɪəsɪz/.
cumulative: /ˈkjuːmjələtɪv/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "큐뮬러티브"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The crisis of fraud and manipulation in peer review has reached a scale that would have been unimaginable a decade ago. The entities that enable scientific fraud at scale — organizations known as "paper mills," which produce fabricated manuscripts on demand and sell authorship positions to researchers under pressure to publish — have grown into a sophisticated industry. A 2025 study published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS) found that fraudulent publications are doubling every 1.5 years, a rate ten times faster than the growth of legitimate scientific literature. These paper mills have learned to game the peer review system by infiltrating journals' editorial boards, compromising the identities of legitimate reviewers, and submitting fake reviews that recommend acceptance of fraudulent manuscripts. The scale of the problem is staggering: more than ten thousand articles were retracted by Hindawi, a Wiley-owned publisher, in 2023 alone, following the discovery of industrialized peer-review manipulation.
sentences.translation: 동료 심사에서의 사기와 조작 위기는 10년 전이라면 상상할 수 없었을 규모에 도달했다. 과학적 사기를 대규모로 가능하게 하는 주체들 — 주문에 따라 조작된 원고를 생산하고 출판 압력을 받는 연구자에게 저자 자격을 판매하는 "논문 공장"이라는 조직 — 은 정교한 산업으로 성장했다. 2025년 미국국립과학원회보(PNAS)에 게재된 연구에 따르면, 사기성 출판물은 1.5년마다 두 배로 늘어나고 있으며, 이는 합법적 과학 문헌 성장 속도의 10배에 달하는 비율이다. 이 논문 공장들은 학술지 편집위원회에 침투하고, 합법적 심사자의 신원을 위조하고, 사기성 원고의 수락을 권고하는 가짜 심사를 제출함으로써 동료 심사 시스템을 악용하는 법을 배웠다. 문제의 규모는 경악할 정도이다: Wiley 소유 출판사인 Hindawi만 해도 산업화된 동료 심사 조작의 발견 이후 2023년에만 1만 건 이상의 논문을 철회했다.

examples: Youth homelessness has reached a scale that would have been unimaginable twenty years ago. The scale of the problem is staggering: over 4.2 million young people experienced homelessness in the past year.

sentences.original: The retraction crisis, as it has come to be known, is both a symptom and a consequence of peer review's failures. When a published paper is found to contain fabricated data, plagiarized text, or other serious integrity violations, the standard remedy is retraction — the formal withdrawal of the paper from the scientific record. But retractions are blunt instruments, applied after the damage has been done, and the process is slow, inconsistent, and often resisted by the authors and institutions involved. An analysis by Retraction Watch and the research integrity community revealed that forty-five editors implicated in a single fraudulent network edited only 1.3 percent of all articles in the affected journals but were responsible for 30.2 percent of all retracted articles — a stunning concentration of malfeasance that illustrates how a small number of compromised individuals can inflict disproportionate damage on the integrity of the scientific literature.
sentences.translation: 철회 위기라고 알려지게 된 것은 동료 심사 실패의 증상이자 결과이다. 출판된 논문에 조작된 데이터, 표절 텍스트, 또는 기타 심각한 무결성 위반이 발견되면, 표준적 구제책은 철회 — 과학적 기록에서 논문의 공식적 철수 — 이다. 그러나 철회는 무딘 도구로서 피해가 이미 발생한 후에 적용되며, 과정은 느리고 일관성이 없으며 관련 저자와 기관에 의해 종종 저항받는다. Retraction Watch와 연구 무결성 커뮤니티의 분석에 따르면, 하나의 사기 네트워크에 연루된 45명의 편집자가 영향받은 학술지 전체 논문의 1.3%만 편집했지만 철회된 전체 논문의 30.2%를 차지했는데, 이는 소수의 훼손된 개인이 과학 문헌의 무결성에 어떻게 불균형적 피해를 입힐 수 있는지를 보여주는 놀라운 비위 집중이다.

examples: The "Great Resignation," as it has come to be known, is both a symptom and a driver of changing workplace expectations.

sentences.notes: 철회 위기를 "증상이자 결과"로 이중 정의하는 문단. "blunt instruments"라는 은유가 철회의 한계를 생생하게 전달하고, 1.3% 대 30.2%라는 극적 대비가 소수 편집자의 불균형적 영향력을 수량적으로 입증한다. "malfeasance"라는 법률적 어조의 단어가 문제의 심각성을 강화한다.

sentences.original: The emergence of artificial intelligence as both a threat to and a potential savior of peer review is one of the most consequential developments in scientific publishing. On the threat side, generative AI tools have made it easier than ever to produce plausible-looking but entirely fabricated manuscripts, complete with realistic data, coherent methodology sections, and properly formatted references. An AI tool that scans manuscript titles and abstracts has flagged more than two hundred and fifty thousand cancer studies alone that bear textual similarities to articles known to have been produced by paper mills — a number that suggests the true scale of the problem is far larger than the retraction statistics indicate. On the potential-savior side, major publishers including Springer Nature, Elsevier, and Wiley are now piloting AI systems for manuscript screening, plagiarism detection, and statistical verification, tools that could dramatically improve the efficiency and consistency of the review process if they prove reliable.
sentences.translation: 인공지능이 동료 심사의 위협이자 잠재적 구원자로 부상한 것은 과학 출판에서 가장 중대한 발전 중 하나이다. 위협 측면에서, 생성형 AI 도구는 실제적인 데이터, 일관된 방법론 섹션, 적절하게 서식화된 참고문헌을 완비한, 그럴듯해 보이지만 완전히 조작된 원고를 그 어느 때보다 쉽게 생산할 수 있게 만들었다. 원고 제목과 초록을 스캔하는 AI 도구는 논문 공장이 생산한 것으로 알려진 논문과 텍스트 유사성을 보이는 암 연구만 25만 건 이상을 표시했는데, 이는 문제의 진정한 규모가 철회 통계가 나타내는 것보다 훨씬 크다는 것을 시사하는 숫자이다. 잠재적 구원자 측면에서, Springer Nature, Elsevier, Wiley를 포함한 주요 출판사들은 현재 원고 선별, 표절 탐지, 통계적 검증을 위한 AI 시스템을 시험 운용하고 있으며, 이는 신뢰할 수 있다고 입증되면 심사 과정의 효율성과 일관성을 극적으로 향상시킬 수 있는 도구이다.

examples: On the risk side, remote work can lead to isolation and burnout. On the opportunity side, it offers unprecedented flexibility and access to global talent.

sentences.original: The question of whether peer reviewers should be paid is one of the most contentious issues in the current reform debate. The traditional model, in which review is an unpaid service, was tenable in an era when the volume of submissions was manageable and the professional incentives were aligned — when reviewing was seen as a privilege rather than a burden, and when the community of researchers in any given field was small enough that reciprocity was the natural norm. That era has passed. The sheer volume of submissions, the pressure to publish, and the absence of any career reward for reviewing have created a situation in which the system depends on goodwill that is rapidly depleting. A 2025 article in PMC argued forcefully for the professionalization of peer review, proposing that reviewers be compensated as "professional referees" with formal training, certification, and remuneration — a model that would increase the cost of publishing but might also increase the quality and reliability of the review process.
sentences.translation: 동료 심사자에게 보수를 지급해야 하는지의 문제는 현재 개혁 논쟁에서 가장 논쟁적인 쟁점 중 하나이다. 심사가 무급 봉사인 전통적 모델은 제출량이 관리 가능하고 전문적 인센티브가 정렬된 시대 — 심사가 부담이 아니라 특권으로 여겨지고, 특정 분야의 연구자 공동체가 상호 호혜가 자연스러운 규범이 될 만큼 충분히 작았던 시대 — 에는 유지 가능했다. 그 시대는 지나갔다. 제출량의 엄청남, 출판 압력, 심사에 대한 경력 보상의 부재는 시스템이 급속히 고갈되고 있는 선의에 의존하는 상황을 만들었다. 2025년 PMC의 논문은 동료 심사의 전문직업화를 강력히 주장하며, 심사자가 공식적 훈련, 인증, 보수를 갖춘 "전문 심판관"으로 보상받을 것을 제안했는데, 이는 출판 비용을 증가시키지만 심사 과정의 질과 신뢰성도 향상시킬 수 있는 모델이다.

examples: The apprenticeship model was tenable in an era when trades were local and demand was stable. That era has passed. Globalization and automation have created a situation in which traditional skills alone no longer guarantee employment.

sentences.original: The concept of "open" or "transparent" peer review has gained considerable traction as an alternative to the traditional anonymous model. In transparent peer review, the reviewers' identities are disclosed to the authors (and sometimes to the public), and the review reports are published alongside the final article, allowing readers to see not just the finished product but the evaluative process that shaped it. Proponents argue that transparency reduces bias, increases accountability, and produces more constructive reviews, because reviewers who know their comments will be public are less likely to be dismissive or reckless. Critics counter that transparency may discourage candid criticism, particularly from junior researchers who fear retaliation from senior colleagues, and that the public scrutiny of reviews could make the already unattractive task of reviewing even less appealing.
sentences.translation: "공개" 또는 "투명" 동료 심사 개념은 전통적 익명 모델의 대안으로 상당한 견인력을 얻었다. 투명 동료 심사에서는 심사자의 신원이 저자에게(때로는 공중에게도) 공개되고, 심사 보고서가 최종 논문과 함께 출판되어 독자가 완성품뿐만 아니라 그것을 형성한 평가 과정도 볼 수 있게 한다. 지지자들은 투명성이 편향을 줄이고, 책임성을 높이며, 더 건설적인 심사를 만들어낸다고 주장하는데, 자신의 논평이 공개될 것을 아는 심사자가 무시적이거나 무책임할 가능성이 적기 때문이다. 비판자들은 투명성이 솔직한 비판을 억제할 수 있다고 반론하는데, 특히 선배 동료의 보복을 두려워하는 후배 연구자의 경우 그러하며, 심사에 대한 공개적 감시가 이미 매력적이지 않은 심사 업무를 더욱 매력 없게 만들 수 있다고 한다.

grammarElements.label: 부사 + 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The idea of universal basic income has gained traction in recent years.
Reviewers should feel free to provide candid feedback without fear of consequences.
Junior faculty members fear retaliation if they criticize senior colleagues publicly.

expressions.usage: 아이디어나 제안이 점점 더 많은 지지와 관심을 받고 있음을 나타내는 표현
반대 입장을 소개할 때 사용하는 학술적 담론 표현

patterns.pattern: Proponents argue that [benefit A], [benefit B], and [benefit C], because [reasoning]. Critics counter that [risk A], particularly [specific group], and that [risk B].
patterns.explanation: 찬성·반대 양측의 논거를 병렬 구조로 균형 있게 제시하는 논쟁 서술 패턴. 학술적 중립성을 유지하면서 양측 모두에 발언권을 준다.
examples: Proponents argue that remote work increases productivity, reduces commuting, and improves work-life balance, because employees have more autonomy. Critics counter that isolation weakens team culture, particularly for new hires, and that blurred boundaries can increase burnout.

sentences.notes: 투명 동료 심사를 둘러싼 찬반 논쟁을 균형적으로 제시하는 문단. "Proponents argue / Critics counter" 구조가 두 입장을 대칭적으로 배치하고, "particularly from junior researchers who fear retaliation"이 반대론의 가장 구체적 근거를 강조한다.

sentences.original: The reproducibility crisis, which has been particularly acute in psychology, biomedicine, and the social sciences, is intimately connected to the limitations of peer review. If the purpose of peer review is to ensure that published research is sound, then the finding that a significant proportion of published results cannot be replicated — estimates range from thirty to seventy percent, depending on the field — represents a devastating indictment of the system's effectiveness. Peer reviewers are not, of course, expected to replicate the experiments they evaluate; they are expected to assess the methodology, the statistical analysis, and the logic of the conclusions. But the reproducibility crisis suggests that these assessments are often insufficient, that reviewers routinely approve papers with underpowered studies, inappropriate statistical methods, or selective reporting of results — flaws that are sometimes difficult to detect from a manuscript alone but that become apparent when the research is subjected to independent replication.
sentences.translation: 심리학, 생의학, 사회과학에서 특히 심각했던 재현성 위기는 동료 심사의 한계와 밀접하게 연결되어 있다. 동료 심사의 목적이 출판된 연구가 건전하다는 것을 보장하는 것이라면, 출판된 결과의 상당 부분이 재현될 수 없다는 발견 — 추정치는 분야에 따라 30%에서 70%에 이른다 — 은 시스템 효과성에 대한 통렬한 비판이 된다. 물론 동료 심사자가 평가하는 실험을 재현하도록 기대되는 것은 아니다; 방법론, 통계 분석, 결론의 논리를 평가하도록 기대된다. 그러나 재현성 위기는 이러한 평가가 종종 불충분하며, 심사자가 검정력이 부족한 연구, 부적절한 통계적 방법, 또는 결과의 선택적 보고가 있는 논문을 일상적으로 승인한다는 것을 시사한다 — 이 결함들은 원고만으로는 때때로 탐지하기 어렵지만 연구가 독립적 재현에 부쳐질 때 명백해진다.

grammarElements.text: which has been particularly acute in psychology, biomedicine, and the social sciences
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절 (분야 한정)

grammarElements.text: If the purpose of peer review is to ensure ... then the finding ... represents
grammarElements.label: 조건문 (If ... then ...)

grammarElements.text: — estimates range from thirty to seventy percent, depending on the field —
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 부연 (통계 범위)

grammarElements.text: flaws that are sometimes difficult to detect ... but that become apparent when
grammarElements.label: 이중 관계절 (대조: sometimes ... but ... when)

vocabulary.example: The reproducibility of experimental results is essential for scientific progress.
The report served as a devastating indictment of the healthcare system.
Underpowered studies are more likely to produce false positive results.

expressions.usage: 두 현상이 깊은 인과적·구조적 관련이 있음을 나타내는 격식적 표현
어떤 시스템이나 관행의 근본적 실패를 강하게 비판하는 표현

patterns.pattern: If the purpose of [system] is to [function], then the finding that [failure data] represents a devastating indictment of [the system's effectiveness].
patterns.explanation: 시스템의 공식적 목적을 전제로 제시하고, 실제 실패 데이터를 대조시켜 체계적 비판을 전개하는 논증 패턴.
examples: If the purpose of standardized testing is to measure learning, then the finding that scores correlate more strongly with family income than with classroom instruction represents a devastating indictment of the system's validity.

sentences.notes: 재현성 위기와 동료 심사의 관계를 분석하는 문단. "If ... then" 조건문이 체계적 비판의 논리 구조를 제공하고, "30~70%"라는 넓은 범위가 문제의 보편성을 강조한다. "not, of course, expected to replicate"라는 양보가 심사자에 대한 비합리적 기대를 인정하면서도 "But"으로 본질적 한계를 지적한다.

pronunciationNotes: reproducibility: /ˌriːprəˌdjuːsəˈbɪlɪti/ — 다섯 번째 음절에 강세. "리프로듀서빌리티"에 가깝다.
indictment: /ɪnˈdaɪtmənt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인다이트먼트"에 가깝다. c는 묵음.

sentences.original: The geographic dimension of peer review's challenges deserves attention, because the system's failures are not distributed equally around the world. China's dominance in AI-related retractions — with five hundred and fifty-one publications retracted, far exceeding any other country — has drawn particular scrutiny and has raised questions about the institutional pressures that drive publication fraud. In China, as in several other countries, academic promotion, funding, and even housing subsidies are tied directly to publication metrics, creating incentives for fraud that overwhelm the ethical norms that peer review depends on. The problem is not unique to China — publication pressure exists everywhere — but the scale and visibility of the Chinese retraction numbers have focused international attention on the systemic conditions that produce fraud, and on the inadequacy of peer review as a defense against it.
sentences.translation: 동료 심사 과제의 지리적 차원은 주목할 가치가 있는데, 시스템의 실패가 전 세계에 균등하게 분포되어 있지 않기 때문이다. AI 관련 철회에서 중국의 우위 — 551건의 출판물이 철회되어 다른 어떤 나라보다 훨씬 앞서는 — 는 특별한 감시를 받았으며 출판 사기를 추동하는 제도적 압력에 대한 의문을 제기했다. 중국에서는, 여러 다른 나라에서와 마찬가지로, 학술적 승진, 연구비, 심지어 주택 보조금까지 출판 지표에 직접 연동되어 있어, 동료 심사가 의존하는 윤리적 규범을 압도하는 사기 유인을 만들어낸다. 문제는 중국에만 고유한 것이 아니다 — 출판 압력은 어디에나 존재한다 — 그러나 중국 철회 숫자의 규모와 가시성은 사기를 생산하는 체계적 조건과, 그에 대한 방어로서 동료 심사의 부적절함에 국제적 관심을 집중시켰다.

grammarElements.label: "not unique to" 양보 + 대시 삽입 + "but" 전환

vocabulary.example: The university's research practices have come under increased scrutiny.
Publication metrics such as the h-index have become central to academic evaluation.
The report highlighted the inadequacy of current regulations.

expressions.usage: 특정 사례가 보편적 현상의 일부임을 강조하며 편견을 방지하는 표현
두 요소 간의 강한 제도적·인과적 연결을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: The problem is not unique to [specific case] — [broader context] — but the [scale/visibility] of [specific case] has focused attention on [underlying issue].
patterns.explanation: 특정 사례를 보편적 맥락에 위치시키면서도, 그 사례의 규모나 가시성 때문에 근본 문제에 관심이 집중된다는 균형 잡힌 분석 패턴.
examples: The problem is not unique to social media — misinformation has always existed — but the speed and reach of online platforms have focused attention on the structural conditions that allow false claims to spread.

sentences.notes: 지리적 불균형을 분석하면서 중국 사례를 구조적 맥락에 위치시키는 문단. "not unique to China"라는 양보가 특정 국가에 대한 편견을 방지하면서, "as in several other countries"가 문제의 보편성을 강조한다. 승진·연구비·주택까지 연결된 인센티브 구조가 사기의 체계적 원인으로 제시된다.

pronunciationNotes: scrutiny: /ˈskruːtɪni/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "스크루터니"에 가깝다.
inadequacy: /ɪnˈædɪkwəsi/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이내디쿼시"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The future of peer review will almost certainly involve a hybrid model that combines human judgment with AI-assisted screening, transparent reporting with some degree of reviewer anonymity, and professional remuneration with continued reliance on voluntary community service. The American Psychological Association's 2025 initiative to strengthen and expand access to scholarly publishing reflects this hybrid vision, emphasizing that the goal is not to replace peer review but to adapt it to the realities of twenty-first-century scientific publishing — a landscape characterized by exponential growth in submissions, increasing sophistication of fraud, declining reviewer availability, and intensifying public demand for scientific reliability.
sentences.translation: 동료 심사의 미래는 거의 확실히 인간의 판단과 AI 보조 선별, 투명한 보고와 일정 정도의 심사자 익명성, 전문적 보수와 자발적 공동체 봉사에 대한 지속적 의존을 결합하는 혼합 모델을 포함할 것이다. 미국심리학회의 2025년 학술 출판 강화 및 접근성 확대 이니셔티브는 이 혼합 비전을 반영하며, 목표가 동료 심사를 대체하는 것이 아니라 21세기 과학 출판의 현실 — 제출물의 기하급수적 증가, 사기의 정교화 증대, 심사자 가용성 감소, 과학적 신뢰성에 대한 공적 요구 심화로 특징지어지는 환경 — 에 적응시키는 것임을 강조한다.

grammarElements.label: 동사구 (미래형 + 확신 부사)

grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 관계절 + 세 쌍의 병렬 결합

grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격 + 과거분사 + 네 항목 병렬

vocabulary.example: Most experts envision a hybrid approach combining tradition and innovation.
The exponential growth of data has outpaced our ability to analyze it.
The increasing sophistication of fraud makes detection more difficult.

expressions.phrase: not to replace ... but to adapt ... to
expressions.usage: 개혁의 성격을 명확히 하여 전면 교체가 아닌 적응적 변화임을 강조하는 표현
복잡한 상황의 주요 특성들을 요약적으로 제시할 때 사용하는 학술적 비유

patterns.pattern: [Future system] will almost certainly involve a hybrid model that combines [A] with [B], [C] with [D], and [E] with [F].
patterns.explanation: 미래 전망을 "almost certainly"로 높은 확신을 표현하면서, 세 쌍의 결합을 병렬하여 혼합적 해결책의 구체적 내용을 제시하는 예측 패턴.
examples: The future of education will almost certainly involve a hybrid model that combines in-person instruction with online resources, standardized assessment with personalized feedback, and professional teaching with AI-powered tutoring.

sentences.notes: 미래 전망을 제시하는 문단. "combines A with B, C with D, and E with F"라는 세 쌍의 병렬이 혼합 모델의 구체적 구성 요소를 제시하고, 대시 뒤 네 항목 병렬(growth, sophistication, availability, demand)이 현재 환경의 도전들을 요약한다. "not to replace but to adapt"이 개혁의 성격을 규정한다.

sentences.original: What the current crisis reveals, more than anything, is that peer review was never designed to do what we have asked it to do. It was designed as a collegial mechanism for improving manuscripts within small, trust-based communities of specialists. It was not designed to serve as the primary defense against industrial-scale fraud, the sole guarantor of scientific reliability, or the universal standard of quality across every discipline, language, and culture on earth. The demands we have placed on the system have exceeded its capacity, and the result is a growing gap between the ideal of peer review — the careful, expert, disinterested evaluation of scientific work — and the reality, which is often rushed, inconsistent, and vulnerable to the very problems it is supposed to prevent.
sentences.translation: 현재 위기가 무엇보다도 드러내는 것은, 동료 심사가 우리가 그것에 요구한 일을 하도록 결코 설계되지 않았다는 것이다. 그것은 소규모의 신뢰 기반 전문가 공동체 내에서 원고를 개선하기 위한 동료적 메커니즘으로 설계되었다. 산업 규모의 사기에 대한 주요 방어, 과학적 신뢰성의 유일한 보증인, 또는 지구상 모든 분야, 언어, 문화에 걸친 보편적 품질 기준으로 기능하도록 설계되지 않았다. 우리가 시스템에 부과한 요구가 그 역량을 초과했으며, 결과는 동료 심사의 이상 — 과학적 연구에 대한 신중하고 전문적이며 사심 없는 평가 — 과 현실 — 종종 서두르고, 일관성이 없으며, 방지해야 할 바로 그 문제들에 취약한 — 사이의 벌어지는 격차이다.

grammarElements.text: What the current crisis reveals, more than anything, is that
grammarElements.label: 의사분열문 (What-cleft: What ... is that ...)

grammarElements.text: was never designed to do what we have asked it to do
grammarElements.label: 동사구 (수동태 + 부정 + 관계절)

grammarElements.text: It was designed as ... It was not designed to serve as ... , ... , or ...
grammarElements.label: 반복적 대비 (was designed / was not designed)

grammarElements.text: the ideal ... — ... — and the reality, which is ...
grammarElements.label: 이상과 현실 대비 + 대시 동격 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The original peer review model was based on collegial trust within small academic communities.
A disinterested evaluation is one free from personal bias or conflict of interest.
The court served as the ultimate guarantor of individual rights.

expressions.phrase: What ... reveals, more than anything, is that
expressions.usage: 가장 핵심적인 통찰을 강조하기 위한 의사분열문 구조

expressions.phrase: a growing gap between the ideal and the reality
expressions.usage: 기대와 실제 사이의 괴리가 심화되고 있음을 나타내는 분석적 표현

patterns.pattern: It was designed as [original purpose]. It was not designed to serve as [expanded demand A], [demand B], or [demand C]. The demands we have placed on [it] have exceeded its capacity.
patterns.explanation: "설계 목적"과 "부과된 요구" 사이의 괴리를 반복적 구조(was designed / was not designed)로 강조하고, "exceeded its capacity"로 구조적 실패를 선언하는 비판 패턴.
examples: Social media was designed as a tool for connecting friends. It was not designed to serve as the primary news source, the arbiter of public discourse, or the engine of political mobilization. The demands we have placed on these platforms have exceeded their capacity.

sentences.notes: 글의 핵심 논지를 집약하는 문단. "What ... reveals, more than anything, is that"이라는 의사분열문이 최종적 통찰을 강조하고, "was designed / was not designed" 반복이 원래 목적과 현재 요구의 괴리를 극적으로 대비시킨다. "the ideal ... and the reality"가 글 전체의 주제를 두 단어 대비로 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a collegial mechanism for improving manuscripts within small, trust-based communities

koreanAlignment.en: a growing gap between the ideal of peer review and the reality
koreanAlignment.ko: 동료 심사의 이상과 현실 사이의 벌어지는 격차

pronunciationNotes: collegial: /kəˈliːdʒiəl/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컬리지얼"에 가깝다.
disinterested: /dɪsˈɪntrəstɪd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "디스인터레스티드"에 가깝다. "interested"의 반대가 아닌 "공정한"이라는 의미에 주의.

sentences.original: Reforming peer review will require not just new tools and new incentives but a fundamental reconceptualization of its role in the scientific enterprise. Peer review is one component of a much larger system of quality control — a system that also includes replication, post-publication review, statistical auditing, and the collective scrutiny of the scientific community over time — and expecting it to bear the entire weight of scientific integrity is neither fair nor realistic. The path forward lies not in defending peer review as it is or in abolishing it in frustration, but in strengthening it where it works, supplementing it where it does not, and being honest about its limitations — the same kind of honesty that science, at its best, demands of everything else.
sentences.translation: 동료 심사를 개혁하려면 새로운 도구와 새로운 인센티브뿐만 아니라 과학적 사업에서의 역할에 대한 근본적 재개념화가 필요할 것이다. 동료 심사는 훨씬 더 큰 품질 관리 시스템의 하나의 구성 요소 — 재현, 출판 후 심사, 통계적 감사, 시간에 걸친 과학 공동체의 집단적 정밀 조사를 포함하는 시스템 — 이며, 그것이 과학적 무결성의 전체 무게를 감당하도록 기대하는 것은 공정하지도 현실적이지도 않다. 나아갈 길은 동료 심사를 현 상태로 옹호하거나 좌절감 속에 폐지하는 데 있지 않고, 작동하는 곳에서 강화하고, 작동하지 않는 곳에서 보완하며, 그 한계에 대해 정직한 데 있다 — 과학이 최선일 때 다른 모든 것에 요구하는 바로 그 종류의 정직함이다.

grammarElements.text: will require not just A and B but C
grammarElements.label: 동사구 (not just A but B 강조 구문)

grammarElements.text: a much larger system of quality control — a system that also includes ...
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격 설명 + 관계절 + 병렬 목적어

grammarElements.text: is neither fair nor realistic
grammarElements.label: 동사구 (neither A nor B 상관접속사)

grammarElements.text: lies not in ... or in ... , but in ... , ... , and ...
grammarElements.label: 동사구 (not in A but in B 세 가지 병렬)

grammarElements.text: — the same kind of honesty that science, at its best, demands of everything else
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격 + 관계절 + 삽입구 (at its best)

vocabulary.example: The crisis demands a fundamental reconceptualization of how we evaluate research quality.
We should focus on supplementing peer review with additional quality control mechanisms.
The collective scrutiny of the scientific community provides an essential check on published findings.

expressions.phrase: not in defending ... or in abolishing ... but in strengthening ... and supplementing
expressions.meaning: 옹호하거나 폐지하는 데가 아니라 강화하고 보완하는 데
expressions.usage: 두 극단을 모두 거부하고 균형 잡힌 중도적 해결책을 제시하는 수사적 표현

expressions.phrase: the same kind of honesty that science demands
expressions.meaning: 과학이 요구하는 바로 그 종류의 정직함
expressions.usage: 과학의 자기비판적 정신을 과학 제도 자체에 적용해야 한다는 자기참조적 마무리 표현

patterns.pattern: The path forward lies not in [extreme A] or in [extreme B], but in [balanced action A], [balanced action B], and [balanced action C] — the same kind of [principle] that [domain], at its best, demands of everything else.
patterns.explanation: 두 극단을 거부하고 세 가지 균형 잡힌 행동을 제시한 뒤, 해당 분야의 핵심 원칙으로 자기참조적 마무리를 만드는 결론 패턴.
examples: The path forward lies not in rejecting technology or in surrendering to it, but in choosing wisely what to automate, what to preserve, and what to reimagine — the same kind of discernment that democracy, at its best, demands of its citizens.

sentences.notes: 글의 결론부. "not just ... but" 구문이 개혁의 범위를 확대하고, "neither fair nor realistic"이 현 기대의 비합리성을 단정한다. "not in ... or in ... but in"이라는 세 갈래 구문이 두 극단(옹호/폐지)을 거부하고 세 가지 균형 잡힌 행동(강화, 보완, 정직)을 제시한다. 마지막 대시 뒤 "the same kind of honesty that science demands"가 과학의 자기비판적 원리를 과학 제도 자체에 적용하는 자기참조적 마무리를 만든다.
